key_insight: Danus scales research-level mathematical reasoning by using a shared fact graph as global memory to coordinate a main agent, parallel workers, and a verifier.
engineering_relevance: The architecture provides a practical template for long-horizon agent systems that need reliable state management, proof traceability, and human-in-the-loop coordination.
method: Main agent plans, monitors progress, and redirects search across workers.
Worker agents run parallel proof search on subproblems.
Stateless verifier checks candidate claims before adding them to the shared fact graph with proofs and dependencies.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports six research-level case studies across algebraic geometry, singularity theory, and combinatorics to show the system can build long, detailed proofs.
watch_item: Performance likely depends on verifier quality and fact-graph consistency, so incorrect admission or dependency tracking could propagate errors through long proof chains.
paper_title: Danus: Orchestrating Mathematical Reasoning Agents with Fact-Graph Memory
